title: Nativity Stable (Fence Picket Craft)
summary: A small three-sided nativity stable cut entirely from one cedar fence picket, with two angled dowel roof supports holding a peaked silhouette.
description:
This whole build comes from a single cedar fence picket — a genuine one-board project. The floor is a plain rectangle, and the two side-back pieces are cut with a 30° angle on one end to form the stable's sloped roofline, giving the silhouette its recognizable peaked-shed shape.

The two front roof-edge pieces are cut with a 30° bevel rather than a square angle, which is what lets the roof edges meet cleanly at the front opening. **Two short dowels, also cut at 30°, brace the roof structure** — small pieces, but they're what holds the peak's shape rather than leaving it to rely on glue alone at the joint.

Assemble with brad nails and glue, then finish with wood stain to bring out the cedar's natural grain — this is a small decorative craft piece, not a structural build, so a light touch with fasteners is enough.

steps.title: Cut the pieces
steps.body: Cut the floor, side-back pieces, front roof edges, and dowel supports from the fence picket.
materials: Cedar fence picket, not dog-eared (5-1/2 in x 72 in)

steps.title: Assemble the stable
steps.body: Attach the side-back pieces to the floor, then add the front roof edges and dowel supports to complete the peaked shape.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply wood stain.
